title: hieracium murorum subsp. lacerisectum
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. lacerisectum, also known as Wall Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The health benefits of Hieracium murorum subsp. lacerisectum are thought to include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used topically as a poultice or salve to aid in wound healing and skin issues.
